title: Software Affected by {{if and .vulnerabilities .exploits}}Known Vulnerabilities and Public Exploits{{else}}{{if .vulnerabilities}}Known Vulnerabilities{{else}}Public Exploits{{end}}{{end}}
summary: Software being used by the affected hosts was detected to be vulnerable to publicly known security issues{{if .exploits}} for which public exploits are available{{end}}.
description:
{{template "summary"}} This may be the result of broader issue, such as a missing or inadequate patching policy for the hosts in scope.

It is important to provide an adequate software upgrade policy to all sensitive environments and not delay the application of security patches more than a few days, as attackers are always keen on searching for new targets every time a security bulletin is published.
recommendations: Incorporate a software patching and upgrade policy to the lifecycle of the product or environment in scope. Ideally, there should be a staging environment that perfectly mirrors the production environment, where patches can be applied and tested safely, and once tests are carried out can be applied to production.
